title: [phase:critique] [family:ui-runtime-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review the current Happy production resolution. Facts: production /happy/ serves updated HTML with Live Session wired to /cockpit; /cockpit returns 200; unauthenticated GET /api/happy/state,/events,/task-detail return 401 by design...
summary_text: 1. **High**: Refresh token rotation is still race-replayable. `rotateRefreshToken` performs `verify -> active-check -> revoke -> issue` as separate DB operations, so concurrent requests with the same old refresh token can each mint a new valid refresh token before revocation is fully serialized ([jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts):267-287, [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts):212-240). 2. **High**: WebSocket auth is point-in-time only. After upgrade, handlers trust cached session `{userId, role}` from tags/memory and do not re-check D1 `is_active`/current role per message, so a user demoted/deactivated after connection can continue privileged WS actions until disconnect ([cockpit-websocket.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/durable-objects/cockpit-websocket.ts):269-287, [cockpit-websocket.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/durable-objects/cockpit-websocket.ts):604-643, [cockpit-websocket.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/durable-objects/cockpit-websocket.ts):1009-1010, [cockpit-websocket.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/durable-objects/cockpit-websocket.ts):1270).
